rulings.Case Title: NAVEEN SINGHA et al VS. NISSIM LANYADOO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 1. PLAINTIFF NAVEEN SINGHA's Motion To Tax Costs GRANTED. Under the circumstances of this case the Court finds that there is no prevailing party for the purposes of recovering costs. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition For Release Of Property From Lien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 2. PLAINTIFF ANTHONY QUESADA's Petition For Release Of Property From Lien GRANTED. No opposition filed. Stipulation to expunge mechanics lien on file. = (501/REQ)

Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 3. DEFENDANTS ADIB KHOURI, ANTELOPE HALAWAN PROPERTIES, LLC, DIANA KHOURI, ISSA KHOURI and FAIQJ KHOURI's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the retaliation cause of action; Plaintiff concedes that demurrer has merit. Demurer is sustained with leave to amend the breach of contract cause of action to allege which express term of the contract is breached. To the extent this cause of action is based on the breach of the implied warranty of habitability the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as breach of warranty of habitability is alleged as the first cause of action. Demurrer is overruled as to (1) the B&P 17200 cause of action, it is sufficiently alleged; (2) nuisance cause of action, it is not duplicative of the negligence cause of action; (3) SFRO 37.3 violation; this cause of action and the facts on which it is based were alleged in the complaint filed on June 20, 2014 before expiration of the statute of limitations; (4) SFRO 37.10B violation is sufficiently alleged. SFRP 37.10B does not have a requirement that the civil action for its violation may only be brought if tenant harassment is a part of a plan to get a tenant to vacate. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLENE FINLEY VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 4. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend to allege facts regarding each element of cause of action for negligence. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to file and serve an amended complaint. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLENE FINLEY VS. SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant San Francisco Housing Authoritys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 4. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY's Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages GRANTED. Plaintiff concedes that the request punitive damages are not properly alleged. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: HARRISON FREMONT HOLDINGS LLC, VS. CAROL RICHARDS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 5. PLAINTIFF HARRISON FREMONT HOLDINGS LLC's Motion To Set Aside Dismissal GRANTED. Defendant breached the stipulation at the time it was in full force and effect. Plaintiff is entitled to possession and daily damages. Plaintiff is entitled to possession. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BALWANTSINH THAKOR VS. THE INDUSTRY GROUP LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication Regarding Duty To Deliver Possession
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 6. PLAINTIFF BALWANTSINH THAKOR's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication Regarding Duty To Deliver Possession GRANTED. Plaintiff shifted its burden. Defendant failed to produce competent admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JOAN KONG VS. MICHAEL COMERFORD ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 7. DEFENDANT MICHAEL COMERFORD's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT overruled, 5 days to answer. First Amended Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support a cause of action for unlawful detainer including requisite relationship and standing; the terms of the agreement and amendments are sufficiently alleged. First Amended Complaint is not uncertain. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EZDEHAR N.ABU AL SHAER VS. EMAD ABU AL SHAER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Continue Trial; Declaratio Nof Ronald S. Galasi
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 10. DEFENDANT EMAD SHAER's Motion To Continue Trial; DeclaratioN of Ronald S. Galasi Off-calendar insufficient notice.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 915 PIERCE, LLC VS. JON MACY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renoticed Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike The Complaints As To Deft Wayne David Hand Or To Convert The Complaint To An Ordinary Civil Action As To Deft Hand
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 8. DEFENDANT WAYNE HAND's Renoticed Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike The Complaints As To Defendant Wayne David Hand Or To Convert The Complaint To An Ordinary Civil Action As To Defendant Hand DENIED: (1) insufficient notice for motion to strike; and (2) bank records are relevant in this case and the need for this evidence outweighs any privacy rights. Parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding redacting the bank records to remove the balance in the accounts and the amounts paid to and by the non-parties to this action. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ESSEX MANAGEMENT CORPORATION VS. ALEX HERNANDEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; Deem Matters Admitted; Compelling Deft To Appear For Oral Deposition; And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, June 12, 2015 line 9. PLAINTIFF ESSEX MANAGEMENT CORPORATION's Motion To Compel Defendantt To Respond To Written Discovery And Produce Documents; Deem Matters Admitted; Compelling Defendant To Appear For Oral Deposition; And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions; GRANTED, subject to opposition. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,060. = (501/REQ)
